patterns.what_to_look_for: TOCTOU double-spend on a balance/credit/quota: code reads a balance, compares it in application memory, then writes a deduction in a separate step with no row lock or atomic decrement, so concurrent requests each pass the check before any commits.
source_indicators: if (account.balance >= amount) { account.balance -= amount; account.save() }
SELECT balance ... then later UPDATE balance, outside one transaction
ORM read-modify-write without select_for_update() / SELECT ... FOR UPDATE
No atomic UPDATE ... SET balance = balance - :amt WHERE balance >= :amt
Missing @Version / optimistic-lock column on wallet or credits model
Redis balance via GET then SET instead of DECR/INCRBY or Lua
blackbox_indicators: N parallel purchase/withdraw requests yield more than one success on a balance that allows one
Final balance goes negative or below intended floor
More credits/items granted than were paid for
Two 200 OKs with identical 'transaction succeeded' for a single-funds action
evidence_required: Pre-state balance snapshot
Burst of K concurrent identical requests (same auth) sent with single-packet/last-byte timing
Per-response status and transaction IDs showing >1 debit accepted
Post-state balance proving the balance>=0 invariant was violated
false_positive_checks: Atomic conditional UPDATE ... WHERE balance >= amount with rows-affected checked
SELECT ... FOR UPDATE inside an explicit transaction
Distributed lock or unique idempotency key dedupes the second request
One request returns 200, the rest 409/422 insufficient-funds (server serializes)
Async reconciliation rejects and reverses the over-debit
patterns.suggested_validation_task: On a funded test account, fire ~20 identical small-purchase requests with HTTP/2 single-packet timing and compare successes against the allowed balance.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as financial double-spend; quantify the over-debit (one balance spent N times) and attach pre/post balance plus the concurrent response transaction IDs.

patterns.what_to_look_for: One-time code / coupon / gift-card / referral redemption-limit bypass: a flow that checks a used flag or redeemed_count < limit, then marks it used in a separate step with no atomic update or unique (code,user) constraint, letting parallel redemptions each see it as unused.
source_indicators: if (!coupon.used) { applyDiscount(); coupon.used = true; coupon.save() }
if (redemptions < maxRedemptions) then increment as a separate statement
No unique constraint on (code, account_id) in the redemptions table
Non-atomic Redis GET+SET for redeemed_count instead of INCR/Lua
Gift-card balance applied via read-modify-write without a lock
blackbox_indicators: Same coupon/gift code applied successfully across multiple parallel carts/orders
Referral or signup credit granted multiple times for one code
'Max uses: 1' yet two parallel requests both return discount applied
Total discount or credit redeemed exceeds the code's face value/cap
evidence_required: The code's stated limit from UI/API (e.g., single-use)
Concurrent burst of identical redeem requests
Multiple success responses each confirming applied discount/credit
Resulting orders/account state showing the limit exceeded
false_positive_checks: DB unique constraint on redemption rejects the duplicate (one 200, rest 409)
Atomic UPDATE ... WHERE used=false (or compare-and-swap) guards the mark
Single success then 'already redeemed' for the remaining requests
Pessimistic lock or per-code queue serializes redemptions
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Issue a single-use test coupon, then submit it on ~15 test carts in parallel and count how many discounts actually apply.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as redemption/limit bypass; show the code's stated cap versus observed redemptions and include the duplicate success responses.

patterns.what_to_look_for: TOCTOU on uniqueness/first-time provisioning: a 'does X exist? no -> create X' path (signup, username/slug claim, default-workspace, first-admin bootstrap) where the existence check and insert are not atomic and no DB unique constraint backs them, so concurrent requests create duplicates or both become 'first'.
source_indicators: if not User.objects.filter(email=...).exists(): User.objects.create(...)
findOne()/count() then create()/insert() with no unique index
if (orgs.count == 0) makeOwner(user) bootstrap path
get_or_create / firstOrCreate without a DB-level unique constraint
No INSERT ... ON CONFLICT / unique index / atomic upsert
blackbox_indicators: Two parallel signups with the same email/username both succeed
Two users both provisioned as org owner/first-admin
Duplicate records (same slug, same invite acceptance) created
Two parallel 'claim handle' requests both return 201
evidence_required: Parallel identical create/claim requests with the same unique field
More than one success or duplicate resource IDs returned
Resulting duplicate rows or duplicate privileged role
Source path showing the check and insert are separate and unguarded
false_positive_checks: DB unique constraint/index present so the second insert errors and app returns 409
Transaction with row/advisory lock around check+insert
One success, others rejected as duplicate
Idempotent upsert (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) collapses the duplicate
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Send two parallel account-create or handle-claim requests with the same unique field using fresh test data and check whether both are created.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as TOCTOU uniqueness bypass; emphasize privilege impact when it yields duplicate owners/admins and attach both success responses.

patterns.what_to_look_for: Rate-limit / OTP / throttle bypass via concurrency: attempt counters (failed-login, OTP/2FA verify, password-reset, SMS send) read-increment-store non-atomically or check the limit before incrementing, so a parallel burst all pass the threshold check simultaneously.
source_indicators: attempts = get(key); if attempts < MAX: ...; set(key, attempts+1)  (GET+SET, not atomic)
Limit checked at request start, counter persisted only after the work
In-memory per-process counter behind multiple workers (no shared atomic store)
OTP verify path with no lock and code not invalidated on first failure
Missing atomic Redis INCR + EXPIRE or Lua script for the counter
blackbox_indicators: N concurrent OTP/2FA guesses accepted beyond the stated max attempts
Login lockout (e.g., 5 tries) bypassed by sending 50 in parallel
More OTP/SMS sends succeed than the documented per-window cap
X-RateLimit-Remaining inconsistent or counts fewer than requests sent
evidence_required: Documented/observed limit (e.g., 5 attempts or 3 OTP sends)
Concurrent burst exceeding the limit with each request marked accepted/processed
Counter or remaining-quota showing fewer than the actual requests sent
For OTP: multiple guesses evaluated against a single live code
false_positive_checks: Atomic INCR-then-check (counter incremented before evaluation)
Single-use code invalidated atomically on the first verify
Edge/WAF rate-limit serializes regardless of app code
Lock or DB-backed atomic counter present
All but the first N return 429/locked
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Against a test account, fire a parallel burst of wrong-OTP or failed-login attempts exceeding the stated cap and count how many are actually evaluated.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as throttle/limit bypass enabling brute force; quantify attempts allowed versus the documented cap and note OTP/2FA brute-force impact.

patterns.what_to_look_for: Status-transition double-processing: an action guarded only by a mutable status field (pending->processed, open->closed) with no idempotency key, atomic CAS, or lock, so concurrent or replayed requests both observe the actionable status and each execute the side effect (double refund, double payout, double ship).
source_indicators: if (order.status == 'pending') { processPayout(); order.status='paid'; save() }
Webhook/callback handler with no idempotency or dedup key
Refund/transfer with no UPDATE ... WHERE status='pending' affected-rows guard
Missing unique constraint on an idempotency_key column
No optimistic version check before the state-changing write
blackbox_indicators: Two parallel refund/withdraw/confirm requests both succeed (double payout)
Replaying a payment/webhook callback triggers the action twice
Two shipment/fulfillment events for one order
Duplicate external side effects (two emails, two ledger entries)
evidence_required: Resource's actionable status before the test
Concurrent or replayed identical action requests
More than one success each confirming the side effect executed
Post-state showing the duplicate effect (two refunds / two ledger rows)
false_positive_checks: Atomic UPDATE ... WHERE status=expected with rows-affected==0 guarding the losers
Idempotency key / dedup table rejects the replay (one 200, rest cached/409)
Pessimistic lock or per-resource queue serializes processing
Optimistic version mismatch rejects the second write
Downstream processor idempotent (PSP dedupes the refund)
patterns.suggested_validation_task: On a test order/refund in actionable state, send two parallel identical action requests plus one replay and check for duplicate side effects.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as double-processing / missing idempotency; show the duplicate side effects and tie them to financial or inventory impact.

patterns.what_to_look_for: Filesystem TOCTOU: a filesystem check (exists/access/stat/owner) is separated from the open/read/write/extract on the same untrusted path, or temp files use predictable names, so a concurrent attacker swaps the path for a symlink between check and use (privileged file overwrite/read).
source_indicators: os.access(path, W_OK) or fs.existsSync(p) then a separate open()/write()
tempfile.mktemp() or predictable /tmp/<name> created then opened later
access()/stat() followed by open() on the same untrusted path
Archive extraction or upload-move that re-stats then writes without O_EXCL/O_NOFOLLOW
chown/chmod applied after create instead of atomic create with mode
blackbox_indicators: Parallel same-name upload/export operations clobber or overwrite each other
Two parallel imports/exports to a predictable path collide or one wins arbitrary content
Error/timing differences indicating a non-atomic open on a shared path
evidence_required: Source path showing check and use on the same name with a window between them
Absence of O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_NOFOLLOW / mkstemp usage
For live: demonstrated file overwrite/clobber via parallel same-name operations
Privilege context of the writing process
false_positive_checks: Atomic open with O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_NOFOLLOW (or mkstemp / NamedTemporaryFile)
Random unguessable temp names in a private sticky-bit directory
Operation runs unprivileged with no symlink-followable target
File descriptor (not path) reused after the check, no re-resolution
Per-operation unique directory prevents name collision
patterns.suggested_validation_task: In a test workspace, trigger parallel same-name upload/export operations and observe whether one clobbers the other or a predictable temp path is reused.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as filesystem TOCTOU, classify as source-root-cause (predictable path / non-atomic open), and tie to privileged file overwrite or info disclosure where the process is privileged.
